title: CMDB Accuracy Scoring
spl:
index=cmdb sourcetype="cmdb:validation" earliest=-7d
| eval ok=if(match(lower(to_string(check_passed)),"(?i)true|1|pass|ok"),1,0)
| stats avg(ok) as accuracy_ratio by ci_class
| eval accuracy_pct=round(accuracy_ratio*100,1)
| sort accuracy_pct
description: Scores accuracy by sampling validation (discovery match, pingable, owner confirmed) — complements completeness-focused quality scores.
value: Scores accuracy by sampling validation (discovery match, pingable, owner confirmed) — complements completeness-focused quality scores.
implementation: Ingest periodic validation (e.g., “IP matches DNS,” “server responds to agent,” “owner replied”). Aggregate pass rate per class and region. Trend monthly for governance scorecards.
visualization: Bar chart (accuracy % by class), Gauge (fleet accuracy), Line chart (trend).
knownFalsePositives: Coverage gaps while onboarding new systems, after mergers, during discovery or field changes, or when teams skip owner updates in busy months.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow` + discovery/network.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `snow:cmdb_ci`, validation job results (`cmdb:validation`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest periodic validation (e.g., “IP matches DNS,” “server responds to agent,” “owner replied”). Aggregate pass rate per class and region. Trend monthly for governance scorecards.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cmdb sourcetype="cmdb:validation" earliest=-7d
| eval ok=if(match(lower(to_string(check_passed)),"(?i)true|1|pass|ok"),1,0)
| stats avg(ok) as accuracy_ratio by ci_class
| eval accuracy_pct=round(accuracy_ratio*100,1)
| sort accuracy_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**CMDB Accuracy Scoring** — Scores accuracy by sampling validation (discovery match, pingable, owner confirmed) — complements completeness-focused quality scores.

Documented **Data sources**: `snow:cmdb_ci`, validation job results (`cmdb:validation`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow` + discovery/network. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cmdb; **sourcetype**: cmdb:validation. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cmdb, sourcetype="cmdb:validation", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ci_class** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **accuracy_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow configuration item or related CMDB view. Spot-check a few CIs, owners, and relationship fields, then confirm discovery dates if we measure freshness.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (accuracy % by class), Gauge (fleet accuracy), Line chart (trend).
grandmaExplanation: We measure how right our key fields are against samples we trust, so quality work has a number to improve.
